mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 60% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return probability above 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative forward returns in greater than 60% of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with tightening monetary policy forces the Federal Reserve to prioritize price stability over growth, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint inflation-tightening signal fails to predict negative returns with a probability exceeding 50% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with expanded corporate profit margins and revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows or positive return probability drops below 50% during GDP expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll growth and unemployment decline signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
